---
title: "Downloads"
---

## All releases
See all of the past releases [on our GitHub](https://github.com/LukeHearon/SeeNote/releases)

##  macOS
### Direct download (.dmg)
<div style="text-align:left;">
  <a href="https://github.com/LukeHearon/SeeNote/releases/latest/download/SeeNote-macOS.dmg">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Download-macOS-000?style=for-the-badge&logo=apple&logoColor=white" alt="Download macOS">
  </a>
</div>

### Homebrew

```sh
brew install --cask LukeHearon/seenote/seenote
```

Or download the `.dmg` above, open it, and drag SeeNote to `/Applications`.

SeeNote is not code-signed, so macOS will block it on first launch. See [macOS woes](#macos-woes) below.

### macOS woes {#macos-woes}

SeeNote is not code-signed. Because of this, macOS will block it on first launch. Here's how to get past it:

1. Install SeeNote to `/Applications` (drag from DMG or use Homebrew).
2. Open SeeNote. You'll see a "SeeNote.app" Not Opened popup. Click "Done".
3. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security, scroll to the Security section near the bottom. You should see ""SeeNote.app" was blocked to protect your Mac". Click "Open Anyway".
4. macOS will show one last dialog titled "Open 'SeeNote.app'?". Click "Open Anyway".

You will only have to do this once. Each time you open a project, you'll need to approve reading from the folder it's contained in.

## Windows
### Direct download (.exe)
<div style="text-align:left;">
  <a href="https://github.com/LukeHearon/SeeNote/releases/latest/download/SeeNote-Windows.exe">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Download-Windows-0078D6?style=for-the-badge&logo=windows&logoColor=white" alt="Download Windows">
  </a>
</div>


## Linux
### Direct download (.AppImage)
<div style="text-align:left;">
  <a href="https://github.com/LukeHearon/SeeNote/releases/latest/download/SeeNote-Linux.AppImage">
    <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Download-Linux-FCC624?style=for-the-badge&logo=linux&logoColor=black" alt="Download Linux">
  </a>
</div>

Download the `.AppImage` above, make it executable, and run it:

```sh
chmod +x SeeNote-Linux.AppImage
./SeeNote-Linux.AppImage
```
